content.full_text:
May 2, 1990

Dear Mr. Bonogan.

We adhered to a telephone conversation on May 1st 1990 to a conflict of interest as a District Board of Directors of Los Moleros Community Hospital.

I had a daughter who had worked at the hospital for 1½ years.

I was appointed to the Board 1½ year ago.

A nepotism policy was forms in april 1, 1987

Scope all Employees (Dept. Employee & Labor Relations

The purpose of the policy is to avoid potential problems arising from the hiring of more than one member of the same family. the 75 designed to allow for maternal flexibility, while also assuring that all incidents of [illegible] a basis a family relationship are balanced

I have talked to the Blanca Berge on May 1st, 1990, and she suggests I write a letter to you to send, there is not a conflict.

Thank you
Helen Hayes
64 Woodward
Deltuny, ca. 94520

[illegible] 415-7524426

This image appears to be a blank lined notebook or notepad page with a yellowish/cream colored background. There is no visible text or content to transcribe on this page.

sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asks whether serving as a District Board of Directors member at Los Medanos Community Hospital while having a daughter employed at the hospital constitutes a conflict of interest under California political practices law.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No formal question/conclusion sections present. Contains a date discrepancy (references May 1st 1980 conversation, but letter dated May 2, 1990). Second page noted as mostly illegible handwritten notes. Document appears incomplete or is missing the FPPC's response.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The requestor asks whether serving as a District Board of Directors member at Los Medanos Community Hospital while having a daughter employed at the hospital constitutes a conflict of interest under California political practices law.
embedding.first_500_words: May 2, 1990 Dear Ms. Borogan, In reference to a telephone conversation on May 1st 1990 to a conflict of interest as a District Board of Director of Los Medanos Community Hospital. I have a daughter who has worked at the hospital for 1/2 year. I was appointed to the Board 1 1/2 years ago. A nepotism policy was formed in April 1, 1987 Scope all Employees (Dept. Employee & Labor Relations) The purpose of this policy is to avoid potential problems stemming from the hiring of more than one member of the same family and is designed to allow for maximum flexibility, while also assuring that all incidents of favoritism or basis a family relationship are avoided. I have talked to Ms. Blanca Breeze on May 1st, 1980, and she suggested I write a letter to you to verify, this is not a conflict. Thank you, Helen Davie 64 Woodward Rd Pittsburg, CA 94565 Home phone 415-432-4426 This page consists entirely of faint, mostly illegible handwritten notes. The handwriting is very faint and mostly illegible, making it difficult to transcribe accurately. There are some scattered letters and numbers that appear to be partially visible, but the majority of the content cannot be reliably read or transcribed.
embedding.summary: Helen Davie, a Board member at Los Medanos Community Hospital, inquires whether her appointment to the board approximately 1.5 years ago conflicts with her daughter's employment at the hospital for approximately 6 months, given the hospital's nepotism policy adopted in April 1987.
